input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Section 1: Dashboard (single page, no tabs) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Section 2: Products (two sub-tabs) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Section 3: Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:

Let me create the files they need.

    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's how it works:

**AppShell.xaml** — Three `FlyoutItem`s in the flyout menu:

| Flyout Entry | Structure |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → one page, no tab bar |
| **Products** | One `Tab` with two `ShellContent` children → renders a **top tab bar** with "Active" and "Archived" |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → one page, no tab bar |

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route so you can navigate to it from either products tab:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

**Key points:**
- `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` = **lazy loading** (pages created only when visited).
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed because `ProductDetailsPage` isn't declared in the XAML hierarchy — it's a detail page you push onto the navigation stack.
- You'll still need to create the page classes (`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, `ProductDetailsPage`) under a `Pages/` namespace.
